One girl dead, another seriously hurt in Val-des-Monts ATV crash   A young girl died and another young girl was injured when the all-terrain vehicle they were riding crashed Thursday evening in Val-des-Monts.

LRT inquiry: Mayor Watson defends receiving more info than council colleagues   Mayor Jim Watson says he believes in “over communicating,” but on Thursday he had to explain why he didn’t let colleagues know that the bar for measuring LRT performance was lowered in the middle of trial running in August 2019.

Counter-protest held downtown as city prepares for return of 'freedom' demonstrators   In the final leg of his cross Canada march to the National War Memorial — in protest of vaccine mandates — James Topp was met with resistance from some Ottawa residents, including members of the local military community.

Victims' advocate wants stalking treated as a 'red flag' crime   The criminal justice system must start treating the crime of stalking as a “red flag” that signals the potential for more serious, violent behaviour, a women’s rights advocate says.
What's open and closed for Canada Day Friday   The Ottawa Citizen and Ottawa Sun don't publish on the holiday but follow us online for breaking news.

Lavell-Harvard: How to mark 'Canada Day'? Let us begin by honouring the land   Use this as a moment to learn whose territory you are standing on, what treaties govern this land and who were its original signatories.

Glavin: The Liberals are funding hate. How else to describe the speakers at this Toronto convention?   Speakers at Muslim Association of Canada gathering will include one who advocates the death penalty for homosexuality and another who says it's OK to beat your wife
Cuthand: Suspension of AFN national chief shows need for change   The executive committee has come down on the national chief like a coup in a banana republic.

MacDougall: Pierre Poilievre isn’t preaching to swing voters, he’s trying to find new ones   In Canada, the 'floating' voter is quickly becoming a thing of the past. In these polarized times, the better play is to find fresh pools of voters.
Editorial: On Canada Day, Ottawa deserves a real party   The town that fun forgot? Ha — we'll see about that.
'LOSING REALLY SUCKS': Redblacks do some good things, but mistakes costly in loss to Lions   Three games into the 2022 CFL season, the Ottawa Redblacks will wonder why they’re now 0-3.
GARRIOCH: The Senators name Trent Mann and Ryan Bowness to take on assistant GM's roles   The Ottawa Senators decided two heads are better than one.

GARRIOCH: Senators GM Pierre Dorion is getting down to business with draft next week   The NHL won’t have its usual fireworks display on Canada Day, but it could signal the beginning of changes to the Ottawa Senators’ roster.
The Ottawa Senators are heading to Belleville to host a pre-season Battle of Ontario   The Ottawa Senators are heading down to the farm in training camp.
LION TAMERS? Redblacks want to prove they're a better team than red-hot B.C.   The Redblacks hope to fix some of the things that have held them back offensively through their first two games.

Meghan bullying claims: Review findings to stay private   LONDON (AP) — Buckingham Palace has investigated how staff handled allegations of bullying made against Meghan, the Duchess of Sussex — but the findings will remain private.

Ottawa Jazz Festival rediscovers its groove   At the halfway point of the rebooted jazz festival, its first full edition since the pandemic started, the music has been plentiful and presented with careful attention to technical details.

Canadians urged to catch up on vaccinations, prepare for fall boosters   Federal health officials are bracing Canadians for another round of COVID-19 vaccines expected to roll out in the fall, and are urging those behind on their boosters to catch up now.
Dining In: Lil Z's Pizza delivers on the basics, shines on creativity   High-quality ingredients and inventive combinations put this pizza joint firmly on the map
Christopher Labos: Myths about vitamins are hard to dislodge   Amazingly, negative vitamin studies don’t seem to dent sales, which amount to billions of dollars globally.
Everyone is complaining about seasonal allergies. What gives?   People are feeling more miserable than usual because the pollen seasons are more robust, says Dr. Anne Ellis

Mike Holmes: Shedding light on garden sheds   A garden shed can be a do-it-yourself project, but it depends on what you want, how big you want it to be, and what your local municipality dictates. While some homeowners may opt for a ready-made shed that just needs assembling, a well-built shed is sturdy and can last for years if built right. However, building a shed has restrictions, and you may need a permit, so always check with your local building authority.

True Crime Byline podcast: The Article that Blew Up a Murder Trial   Listen to this podcast episode for the shocking story of how a National Post article about a teen vampire led to a mistrial, as told by reporter Joseph Brean

Douglas Todd: Canadian cops far less likely to kill, or be killed, than U.S. counterparts   Analysis: Canadian police are often accused of using excessive force, but a U.S. officer is five times more likely than a Canadian cop to shoot and kill.

True Crime Byline: Families knew 'horrible creature' was taking Vancouver women long before police did   The DNA of 33 missing women was found on Robert Pickton's pig farm. But it's the story of the victims that stays with me, not the killer.

True Crime Byline podcast: This Isn't a Story about Robert Pickton   Listen to this podcast episode for the heartbreaking history of Canada’s most prolific serial killer case, as told by Vancouver Sun reporter Lori Culbert.

Nissan recalls about 323K SUVs; hoods can open unexpectedly   WASHINGTON (AP) — Nissan is recalling nearly 323,000 Pathfinder SUVs in the U.S. because the hoods can unexpectedly fly open and block the driver’s view.

German court doubles red-light runner's fine due to SUV size   The penalty was set higher because the sport-ute allegedly posed more risk to pedestrians than a smaller car
Cross-Canada Road Trip: Whitehorse to Toronto in two 2022 Toyota Tundras   Some 5,500 km and seven days of driving through one magnificent country show the new-gen truck is just as stout as the old

Justin Bieber 'did something very important for the facial difference community'   Advocate Alim Somji says the pop star challenges the typical images of facial difference portrayed in the media as villains, such as Scar from the movie The Lion King.

WATCH: We take our medications out of the bathroom thanks to this TikTok trend   A bathrooms isn't a great storage option for meds, as the swings in temperatures and moist conditions can cause them to lose potency.
What are the signs of pancreatitis?   Acute and chronic pancreatitis are typically diagnosed between the ages of 40 and 70, with men more likely to be diagnosed across all age groups.

Machado: COVID has made socializing a little like having sex without protection   Getting back to some kind of normal means accepting the risk of COVID, and understanding that the feeling of liberation comes along with fear and uncertainty.
What are the signs of Parkinson's disease?   For people living with Parkinson's disease, exercise and eating a balanced diet may help control symptoms and even slow progression of the disease.
Ontario man accused of impaired driving after cannabis and alcohol found inside his vehicle   Man taken into custody after the OPP received multiple complaints about his driving on Highway 12
Over half of people using medical cannabis to combat chronic pain had withdrawal symptoms: study   Clusters of symptoms occur after significant periods of not using marijuana.
Florida man's idea to hide two cannabis cigars in his shoe was a bust   Man arrested for weed possession and driving with a suspended licence.

Toronto-based company partners with Wiz Khalifa on line of psychedelic and functional mushrooms   While the psychedelic mushrooms will only be available in the Netherlands, functional home-grow kits are set to launch in the U.S., Europe and Canada.

Lawyer looking to secure cannabis retail spot for client tried to bribe police chief   The lawyer approached a close relative of the police chief and offered that person $32,250 to speak to the chief.
